perturbation.original_text: 2.2 The seller shall be confirmed within three working days after receipt of order. If the seller finds order is not acceptable or need to modify, should note entrusted party in two working days after receipt of the order, If the seller did not confirm orders in time or notice not accept orders or modifications, the seller is deemed to have been accepted the order. The orders become effective once the seller accepts, any party shall not unilaterally cancel the order before the two sides agreed .
perturbation.changed_text: 2.2 The seller shall be confirmed within three working days after receipt of order. If the seller finds order is not acceptable or need to modify, should note entrusted party in two working days after receipt of the order, If the seller did not confirm orders in time or notice not accept orders or modifications, the seller is deemed to have been accepted the order. The orders become effective once the seller accepts, any party shall unilaterally cancel the order at any time, regardless of prior agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the text to allow either party to unilaterally cancel the order at any time, it directly contradicts the earlier statement in the same paragraph that "any party shall not unilaterally cancel the order before the two sides agreed." This contradiction creates uncertainty about the enforceability of the order and the rights of each party.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2.2 The seller shall be confirmed within three working days after receipt of order. If the seller finds order is not acceptable or need to modify, should note entrusted party in two working days after receipt of the order, If the seller did not confirm orders in time or notice not accept orders or modifications, the seller is deemed to have been accepted the order. The orders become effective once the seller accepts, any party shall unilaterally cancel the order at any time, regardless of prior agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that 'any party shall unilaterally cancel the order at any time, regardless of prior agreement.' This directly contradicts the original text in the same section, which states that 'any party shall not unilaterally cancel the order before the two sides agreed.' The change introduces a direct conflict within the same paragraph, creating a clear in-text contradiction and uncertainty about the enforceability of the order and the rights of each party. This aligns with the definition of 'Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction' because the same clause, when modified, presents conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: 11. PAYMENT: Under Letter of Credit or T/T: Under the Letter of Credit: The Buyer shall open an irrevocable letter of credit with the bank within 30 days after signing the contract, in favor of the Seller, for 100% value of the total contract value. The letter of credit should state that partial shipments are allowed. The Buyer's agent agrees to pay for the goods in accordance with the actual amount of the goods shipped. 80% of the system value being shipped will be paid against the documents stipulated in Clause 12.1. The remaining 20% of the system value being shipped will be paid against the documents stipulated in Clause 12.2. The Letter of Credit shall be valid until 90 days after the latest shipment is effected. Under the T/T The trustee of the buyer remitted the goods to the seller by telegraphic transfer in batches as agreed upon after signing each order.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. PAYMENT: Under Letter of Credit or T/T: Under the Letter of Credit: The Buyer shall open an irrevocable letter of credit with the bank within 30 days after signing the contract, in favor of the Seller, for 50% value of the total contract value. The letter of credit should state that partial shipments are allowed. The Buyer's agent agrees to pay for the goods in accordance with the actual amount of the goods shipped. 80% of the system value being shipped will be paid against the documents stipulated in Clause 12.1. The remaining 20% of the system value being shipped will be paid against the documents stipulated in Clause 12.2. The Letter of Credit shall be valid until 90 days after the latest shipment is effected. Under the T/T The trustee of the buyer remitted the goods to the seller by telegraphic transfer for 100% value in full after signing this contract.
perturbation.explanation: The original text states that the Letter of Credit should be for 100% of the total contract value. By changing it to 50%, it contradicts the initial instruction. Furthermore, by adding 'The trustee of the buyer remitted the goods to the seller by telegraphic transfer for 100% value in full after signing this contract.', it contradicts the first part of the sentence 'Under Letter of Credit or T/T', making it unclear which payment method should be used.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. PAYMENT: Under Letter of Credit or T/T: Under the Letter of Credit: The Buyer shall open an irrevocable letter of credit with the bank within 30 days after signing the contract, in favor of the Seller, for 50% value of the total contract value. The letter of credit should state that partial shipments are allowed. The Buyer's agent agrees to pay for the goods in accordance with the actual amount of the goods shipped. 80% of the system value being shipped will be paid against the documents stipulated in Clause 12.1. The remaining 20% of the system value being shipped will be paid against the documents stipulated in Clause 12.2. The Letter of Credit shall be valid until 90 days after the latest shipment is effected. Under the T/T The trustee of the buyer remitted the goods to the seller by telegraphic transfer for 100% value in full after signing this contract.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a direct contradiction. Initially, the Letter of Credit was to be for 100% of the contract value, as stated in the original text of Section 11. By changing this to 50%, and simultaneously stating that the trustee will remit 100% via T/T 'after signing this contract', the document creates an irreconcilable conflict regarding the payment method and the amount to be covered by the Letter of Credit. This also directly contradicts the first part of the sentence 'Under Letter of Credit or T/T', creating ambiguity and inconsistency.

perturbation.original_text: 16. CLAIMS: Within 7 days after the arrival of the goods at destination, should the quality, specification, or quantity be found not in conformity with the stipulations of the Contract except those claims for which the insurance company or the owners of the vessel are liable, the Buyers, on the strength of the Inspection Certificate issued by the China Commodity Inspection Bureau, have the right to claim for replacement with new goods, or for compensation, and all the expenses (such as inspection charges, freight for returning the goods and for sending the replacement, insurance premium, storage and loading and unloading charges etc.) shall be borne by the Sellers. The Certificate so issued shall be accepted as the base of a claim. The Sellers, in accordance with the Buyers' claim, shall be responsible for the immediate elimination of the defect(s), complete or partial replacement of the commodity or shall devaluate the commodity according to the state of defect(s). Where necessary, the Buyers shall be at liberty to eliminate the defect(s) themselves at the Sellers' expenses. If the Sellers fail to answer the Buyers within one weeks after receipt of the aforesaid claim, the claim shall be reckoned as having been accepted by the Sellers.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. CLAIMS: Within 7 days after the arrival of the goods at destination, should the quality, specification, or quantity be found not in conformity with the stipulations of the Contract except those claims for which the insurance company or the owners of the vessel are liable, the Buyers, on the strength of any evidence they deem sufficient, have the right to claim for replacement with new goods, or for compensation, and all the expenses (such as inspection charges, freight for returning the goods and for sending the replacement, insurance premium, storage and loading and unloading charges etc.) shall be borne by the Buyers. The Certificate so issued shall be accepted as the base of a claim. The Sellers, in accordance with the Buyers' claim, shall be responsible for the immediate elimination of the defect(s), complete or partial replacement of the commodity or shall devaluate the commodity according to the state of defect(s). Where necessary, the Buyers shall be at liberty to eliminate the defect(s) themselves at the Sellers' expenses. If the Sellers fail to answer the Buyers within one weeks after receipt of the aforesaid claim, the claim shall be reckoned as having been accepted by the Sellers.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause requires an Inspection Certificate from the China Commodity Inspection Bureau to make a claim. By changing "the Inspection Certificate issued by the China Commodity Inspection Bureau" to "any evidence they deem sufficient," the modified version allows claims based on any evidence the buyer chooses, directly contradicting the requirement for a specific certificate. Furthermore, By changing "all the expenses shall be borne by the Sellers" to "all the expenses shall be borne by the Buyers", it contradicts where all the expenses should be paid.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 16. CLAIMS: Within 7 days after the arrival of the goods at destination, should the quality, specification, or quantity be found not in conformity with the stipulations of the Contract except those claims for which the insurance company or the owners of the vessel are liable, the Buyers, on the strength of any evidence they deem sufficient, have the right to claim for replacement with new goods, or for compensation, and all the expenses (such as inspection charges, freight for returning the goods and for sending the replacement, insurance premium, storage and loading and unloading charges etc.) shall be borne by the Buyers. The Certificate so issued shall be accepted as the base of a claim. The Sellers, in accordance with the Buyers' claim, shall be responsible for the immediate elimination of the defect(s), complete or partial replacement of the commodity or shall devaluate the commodity according to the state of defect(s). Where necessary, the Buyers shall be at liberty to eliminate the defect(s) themselves at the Sellers' expenses. If the Sellers fail to answer the Buyers within one weeks after receipt of the aforesaid claim, the claim shall be reckoned as having been accepted by the Sellers.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a contradiction. The original text mandates claims be based on an inspection certificate from the China Commodity Inspection Bureau. The changed text broadens this, allowing claims based on 'any evidence they deem sufficient,' directly conflicting with the original requirement. Also, the original text says expenses are borne by the seller, and the changed text changes that to the buyers, which creates a contradiction. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text in this case.
